texto: The Left has made the young gullible and materialist, while the Right has turned them into nihilistic cynics. This paradigm of power and struggle, expressed through endless ego-driven duality games will eventually lose steam. The new methodology must reconcile these two extremes by formulating a way of living that embodies the virtues of a natural, universal being - simplicity, patience, and compassion.
explicacao: Orientação direta de VD. sobre temas ligados a vida. A afirmação “The Left has made the young gullible and materialist, while the Right has turned them into nihili…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, government, progress, útil em momentos de termino e progress.

texto: The scientist is more religious than the poet because his belief rests in the faith of the intangible –– a reality that stands outside of ourselves who are the observers. The poet on the other hand realizes that concepts like Truth and Beauty lie not in the fleeting nature of our world, but in the eternal aspects of humanity which form the root of our reality and identities.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a VD.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a fe. O trecho “The scientist is more religious than the poet because his belief rests in the faith of the intang…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, human, religion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de religion e science.

texto: The scientist is more religious than the poet because his beliefs rests in the faith of a reality that is intangible, one that stands outside of the observer. The poet on the other hand, concentrates his attention on the source of the experience, because he realizes that true identities lie not in the fleeting nature of our world, but in the eternal aspects of humanity that is the root of this reality and of ourselves .
